primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer, au regard d’IFRS 10, si un investisseur détient le pouvoir sur une entité au titre de l’évaluation du contrôle

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that the standard directly governing control and power over an investee is IFRS 10, so it supersedes any lower-precedence or non-directly-governing IAS guidance and displaces overlapping control references in other documents for this issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty, because the question is explicitly about whether an investor has power over an entity, which is directly governed by IFRS 10.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern how power is identified as part of the IFRS 10 control assessment.

supporting_authority.reason: This agenda decision clarifies reassessment of control when previously protective rights become exercisable.
This agenda decision illustrates how to identify relevant activities and assess power in structured lease vehicles.

treatment_families.family: Power obtained through voting rights that direct the relevant activities
Power obtained through contractual or other decision-making rights when voting rights are not the dominant factor
Power assessed through delegated decision-making rights by determining whether the decision maker is a principal or an agent
Power evidenced by practical ability and relationships with other parties, including de facto agents

approaches.label: Voting rights
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 identifies voting rights, including substantive and potential voting rights, as a core top-level way in which power may arise.

approaches.label: Contractual rights
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 provides a distinct top-level model in which power arises from contractual or other rights rather than from voting rights.

approaches.label: Principal-agent assessment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Where decision-making rights are delegated, IFRS 10 requires a distinct principal-versus-agent assessment to determine whether the decision maker has power.

approaches.label: De facto power
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 10 separately recognizes situations in which practical ability and relationships with other parties provide evidence that an investor can direct relevant activities unilaterally.
